author: @WalterBurton
text:
I don't know if this is helpful, but I imagine I'm not the only one who just puts videos in their Watch Later queue and views them more-or-less sequentially. 

Which is to say, if I like what I see, then I'll subscribe to that channel, and then I'll get notifications, and then I'll add videos to my Watch Later queue, rinse and repeat. I won't navigate to sub-channels or other channels or whatever. Not on my own. 

Subscribing to a channel always comes from watching a video. For me. This is just a semi-sober observation that might or might not be helpful when it comes to understanding the tiny part of your demographic represented by me and my habits (quirks?). Cheers! Keep up the great work; please and thank you! :-) 👍👍👍

author: @DIYGUY1-2-3
text: Hi...Can I use old battery powered drill motors to generate power. I checked one of mine, (18v) by connecting a 230v drill to it with a piece of round bar, chuck to chuck and found that it does generate but I had to reverse the positive and negative wires to my tester. I also cuts in and out. How can I perfect this and I think the gearbox would best be removed?

author: @chriskeeble
text:
Sounds too complicated.

One channel, free subscription and members only restricted videos.

Playlists for topics / projects. Then we can search in one place.

Just my thoughts and feedback.

author: @angelusmendez5084
text:
Great idea! 👏

I was thinking it'd be interesting to have a "reaction" section in the twitch streams.

Where you (or someone else) pick an old video of your channel and react to it. 

So we can know your current thoughts on old interesting ideas. 

I just thought might be interesting...

Cheers
